sections.paragraph:
συστενάζω to groan together with, lament, groan (Eur., Ion 935 and TestIss 17:5 τινί ‘with someone’.—Nicetas Eugen. 1, 342 H. without dat.) of creation groan together (w. συνωδίνειν, q.v. Also A-MDubarle, RSPT 38, ’54, 445–65) Ro 8:22 (on the pessimistic tone s. GKennedy, The Art of Rhetoric in the Roman World ’72, 447f).—DELG s.v. στένω. TW.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Nicetas Nicetas Eugen , XII a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

dat. dat. = dative

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

RSPT RSPT = Revue des sciences philosophiques et théologiques—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 978
